enabled.description: Set to false to disable the persistence item.

globalMounts.description:
Configure mounts to all controllers and containers.
By default the persistence item will be mounted to /<name_of_the_persistence_item>.
Each entry supports: path, readOnly, subPath, subPathExpr, and mountPropagation.

advancedMounts.description:
Explicitly configure mounts for specific controllers and containers.
Structure: advancedMounts.<controller_name>.<container_name>[].path
Use this instead of globalMounts when different controllers need different mount configurations.

item.type.persistentVolumeClaim.description: Dynamically provision a PersistentVolumeClaim. This is the default persistence type and will be used when no type is specified.
examples:
persistence:
  config:
    type: persistentVolumeClaim
    accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
    size: 1Gi

persistence:
  config:
    type: persistentVolumeClaim
    accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
    size: 10Gi
    storageClass: fast-ssd
    retain: true
    globalMounts:
      - path: /config

accessMode.description:
AccessMode for the persistent volume. Make sure to select an access mode that is supported by your storage provider!
See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes/#access-modes

dataSource.description:
The optional data source for the persistentVolumeClaim.
See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes/#volume-populators-and-data-sources

dataSourceRef.description:
The optional volume populator for the persistentVolumeClaim.
See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes/#volume-populators-and-data-sources

retain.description: Set to true to retain the PVC upon helm uninstall.

size.description: The amount of storage that is requested for the persistent volume.

storageClass.description:
Storage Class for the config volume.
If set to '-', dynamic provisioning is disabled.
If set to something else, the given storageClass is used.
If undefined (the default) or set to null, no storageClassName spec is set, choosing the default provisioner.

item.type.persistentVolumeClaimExisting.description: Attach an existing PersistentVolumeClaim instead of creating a new one.
examples:
persistence:
  config:
    type: persistentVolumeClaim
    existingClaim: my-app-data

existingClaim.description:
The name of an existing PersistentVolumeClaim to use. Helm templates are supported.
This will not create a new PVC, but use the existing one instead.

item.type.configMap.description: Mount a ConfigMap as a volume. Use 'name' for external ConfigMaps, or 'identifier' to reference one managed by this chart.
examples:
persistence:
  config:
    type: configMap
    name: my-configmap

persistence:
  config:
    type: configMap
    identifier: my-chart-configmap
    globalMounts:
      - path: /etc/app/config
        readOnly: true

persistence:
  config:
    type: configMap
    name: my-configmap
    items:
      - key: settings.yaml
        path: settings.yaml
item.type.configMap.$comment: oneOf enforces that at least one of name/identifier is present. dependentSchemas enforces mutual exclusion with a targeted error when both are set. Both are needed — do not simplify to oneOf alone.

item.type.secret.description: Mount a Secret as a volume. Use 'name' for external Secrets, or 'identifier' to reference one managed by this chart.
examples:
persistence:
  credentials:
    type: secret
    name: my-secret

persistence:
  tls:
    type: secret
    name: app-tls-cert
    globalMounts:
      - path: /etc/ssl/certs
        readOnly: true
item.type.secret.$comment: oneOf enforces that at least one of name/identifier is present. dependentSchemas enforces mutual exclusion with a targeted error when both are set. Both are needed — do not simplify to oneOf alone.

item.type.nfs.description: Mount an NFS share as a volume.
examples:
persistence:
  media:
    type: nfs
    server: 192.168.1.100
    path: /mnt/share/media

persistence:
  backups:
    type: nfs
    server: nas.example.lan
    path: /volume/Backups/k8s/app
    globalMounts:
      - path: /backups

path.description: The NFS export path on the server.

server.description: The NFS server hostname or IP address.

item.type.emptyDir.description: Use an emptyDir volume for scratch space or sharing data between containers in the same pod. Data is lost when the pod is removed.
examples:
persistence:
  tmp:
    type: emptyDir

persistence:
  cache:
    type: emptyDir
    medium: Memory
    sizeLimit: 256Mi

medium.description: Storage medium. Set to 'Memory' for tmpfs (RAM-backed). Leave empty for node disk.

sizeLimit.description: Maximum size of the volume (e.g. '1Gi'). Only enforced for tmpfs.

item.type.ephemeral.description: Use a generic ephemeral volume for scratch space or sharing data between containers. The volume is created and deleted with the pod.
examples:
persistence:
  cache:
    type: ephemeral

persistence:
  cache:
    type: ephemeral
    accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
    storageClass: fast-ssd
    size: 10Gi

accessMode.description:
AccessMode for the ephemeral volume. Make sure to select an access mode that is supported by your storage provider!
See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes/#access-modes

storageClass.description:
Storage Class for the ephemeral volume.
If set to '-', dynamic provisioning is disabled.
If set to something else, the given storageClass is used.
If undefined (the default) or set to null, no storageClassName spec is set, choosing the default provisioner.

size.description: The amount of storage that is requested for the ephemeral volume.

item.type.hostPath.description: Mount a path from the host node into the pod. Use with caution as it ties the pod to a specific node.
examples:
persistence:
  dev-tun:
    type: hostPath
    hostPath: /dev/net/tun
    hostPathType: CharDevice

persistence:
  data:
    type: hostPath
    hostPath: /mnt/local-data
    hostPathType: DirectoryOrCreate

hostPath.description: Path on the host node to mount.

hostPathType.description: Type of the host path. Values: DirectoryOrCreate, Directory, FileOrCreate, File, Socket, CharDevice, BlockDevice.

item.type.image.description: Mount OCI image or artifact contents as a read-only volume. Requires Kubernetes 1.31+ with the ImageVolume feature gate.
examples:
persistence:
  static-assets:
    type: image
    image: registry.example.com/assets:v1.0

persistence:
  tools:
    type: image
    image:
      repository: registry.example.com/tools
      tag: latest
    pullPolicy: Always

image.description: OCI artifact reference to be used.

pullPolicy.description: The image pull policy for the image persistence item.

item.type.custom.description: Specify a custom volume type not covered by the built-in types. The volumeSpec is passed through directly to the pod spec.
examples:
persistence:
  ceph:
    type: custom
    volumeSpec:
      cephfs:
        monitors:
          - 10.0.0.1:6789
        user: admin
        secretRef:
          name: ceph-secret

persistence:
  projected:
    type: custom
    volumeSpec:
      projected:
        sources:
          - secret:
              name: my-secret
          - configMap:
              name: my-config

volumeSpec.description: Raw Kubernetes volume specification. Passed through directly to the pod spec volumes list.

globalMounts.description: Mount the volume to all controllers and containers. Each entry specifies a mount point.
examples:
globalMounts:
  - path: /config

globalMounts:
  - path: /data
    readOnly: true
  - path: /data/subdir
    subPath: subdir

advancedMounts.description: Mount the volume to specific controllers and containers. Use when different containers need different mount configurations.
examples:
advancedMounts:
  main:
    main:
      - path: /config
    sidecar:
      - path: /shared
        readOnly: true

path.description:
The path where the volume should be mounted in the container. Helm templates are supported.
If not specified, the mount path will be set to /<identifier_of_the_peristence_item>.

subPath.description:
The subPath within the volume to mount. Helm templates are supported.
If not specified, the entire volume will be mounted.
